name: Dark Chocolate

book_claims.claim: Plain dark chocolate (rich in epicatechin) increases plasma antioxidant capacity. Effect markedly reduced when consumed with milk.
book_claims.mechanism: Epicatechin is a potent antioxidant flavonoid. Milk proteins bind to epicatechin preventing absorption.

supplementary_research.finding: Cochrane meta-analysis of 20 RCTs found chocolate/cocoa consumption significantly reduced blood pressure (systolic -2.8 mmHg, diastolic -2.2 mmHg) and improved flow-mediated dilation. Effects attributed to flavanols, particularly epicatechin.

Pure epicatechin from cocoa increased plasma nitric oxide levels and improved endothelial function in humans. Adding milk to cocoa abolished these effects by binding epicatechin, directly confirming the book's claim about dairy interference.
Dark chocolate (100g/day, >70% cocoa) improved insulin sensitivity, reduced blood pressure, and improved flow-mediated dilation in hypertensive patients with impaired glucose tolerance over 15 days.

Strong evidence for blood pressure reduction and improved blood flow. Moderate evidence for LDL reduction and insulin sensitivity improvement. Epicatechin and other flavanols are the primary active compounds. Minimum 200 mg cocoa flavanols/day for cardiovascular benefit.

culinary_pairings.source: Mocha; shared flavanol and chlorogenic acid compounds
Author combines cocoa powder with brazil nuts in morning smoothies
Shared polyphenol profiles; classic flavour affinity

seasonality: Available year-round. Cocoa beans harvested twice yearly in tropical regions. Choose dark chocolate with >70% cocoa solids for maximum flavanol content. Minimally processed (non-Dutch-process) cocoa powder retains more flavanols.

nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Well absorbed in absence of dairy proteins. Milk proteins bind epicatechin and prevent absorption -- consume dark chocolate without milk. Peak plasma levels at 2 hours post-ingestion.
Mild stimulant (weaker than caffeine). Vasodilator. Half-life ~7 hours. Responsible for mood-elevating effects.

nutrient_highlights.amount_per_100g: 170-560 mg (dark chocolate; varies enormously by processing)
nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Dutch processing (alkalisation) destroys up to 90% of flavanols. Choose non-alkalised cocoa and minimally processed dark chocolate.
Meaningful dietary source. Important for cardiovascular function and insulin sensitivity.

synergies.description: Author combines dark cocoa powder with brazil nuts in morning smoothies
Shared chlorogenic acid family; complementary flavanol profiles

warnings: Never consume with milk or dairy -- milk proteins bind epicatechin and abolish antioxidant effects (Nature study)
Dutch-processed (alkalised) cocoa loses up to 90% of flavanols -- choose natural/non-alkalised
Small amounts only; dark chocolate is calorie-dense (~550 kcal/100g)
